extra.reasoning: slurred_speech appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100) IF (chest_pain AND fever AND severe_headache AND rash AND slurred_speech) THEN acuity := self_care. No rule raises acuity from slurred_speech; it can only enable this highest-priority drop to self_care. Counterexample input: chest_pain=T, fever=T, severe_headache=T, rash=T, back_pain=F, sweating=F, hemoptysis=F. With slurred_speech=FALSE: interaction_drop does not fire; matching rules are raise_chest_pain (p2->urgent), raise_severe_headache (p2->urgent), raise_fever (p3->emergent), raise_rash (p3->emergent). Highest priority is p3, yielding acuity=emergent. With slurred_speech=TRUE (everything else unchanged): interaction_drop (p100) now fires and, having the highest priority, wins -> acuity=self_care. Acuity drops from emergent to self_care when slurred_speech is set true, violating the monotonicity property (setting slurred_speech true never lowers acuity). The witness_assignment lists the slurred_speech=true state that produces the lower acuity.
